none.justification: Matches the GT on the key shared state: per-worker bio_jobs and bio_jobs_counter protected by bio_mutex[worker], bio_comp_list protected by the separate bio_mutex_comp, job_comp_pipe with no mutex, the three redisAtomic AOF fields, and immutable server.bio_cpulist. It also correctly excludes ownership-transferred job payloads and other non-shared items.

ccodegraph.justification: Covers all GT-required shared state and synchronization, including the per-worker mutex granularity, separate completion-list mutex, no-mutex pipe, redisAtomic fields, and immutable bio_cpulist. It slightly over-discusses job payload ownership transfer, but identifies it as having no independent concurrent sharing, so there is no material miss.

codegraph.justification: Mostly matches the inventory and catches the important dimensions: per-worker bio_mutex, separate bio_mutex_comp, pipe without mutex, atomics, and bio_cpulist. However it incorrectly folds the startup plain assignment of fsynced_reploff_pending into atomic-protected accesses and describes atomics as possibly falling back to a mutex, conflicting with the GT's lock-free atomic nuance.

cbm.justification: Gets the core shared state and protections right, including bio_jobs/counters, bio_comp_list, atomic AOF fields, job_comp_pipe, and bio_cpulist. But it over-includes explicit GT exclusions such as bio_worker_title/bio_job_to_worker as shared state and gives an incomplete pipe-safety explanation, omitting the POSIX 1-byte write atomicity point.
